title: Regression in allowed container names breaks Fedora 30 SilverBlue
body:
/kind bug

In the old commit from 2018 de6d5b75ac8baa6871991984dc8dcf9b702b8a9e libpod added a regex to check the container name, which was then changed in https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/2793 to be a little less restrictive. Something more recent than this started then broke fedora-toolbox (and thus Fedora SilverBlue) as it uses container names like `fedora-toolbox-hughsie:30` i.e. with a colon before the tag. I've not tracked down the exact commit that is responsible. We did think `:` was a valid char as it's used heavily in the OCI spec...

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install Fedora 30 SilverBlue
2. toolbox create

**Describe the results you received:**

    toolbox: trying to create container fedora-toolbox-hughsie:30
    Error: error running container create option: name must match regex [a-zA-Z0-9_-]+: invalid argument

**Describe the results you expected:**

A toolbox with the name `fedora-toolbox-hughsie:30`

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Version:            1.2.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.12
  podman version: 1.2.0
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.7.2

    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.12.0-dev, commit: a13eadcaf0f07ba590c96d845a03ac3a0da7c56d'
  Distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "30"
  MemFree: 12024987648
  MemTotal: 16411090944
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-75.dev.git751f18d.fc30.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc6+dev
      commit: 34cd853bfa9dc792faaae59bbb4d3020225c98c8
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  SwapFree: 8270114816
  SwapTotal: 8270114816
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 64
  hostname: localhost.localdomain
  kernel: 5.0.5-300.fc30.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  uptime: 28m 37.99s
insecure registries:
  registries: []

  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org

  ConfigFile: /home/hughsie/.config/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 1
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
  GraphRoot: /var/home/hughsie/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 2
  RunRoot: /run/user/1000
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Physical server, Fedora 30 SilverBlue.

comments.body:
Wait, we previously allowed `-` and `:`? That sounds like a bug to me. The original intent of the regex was alphanumeric characters and `_`

I have no issues with allowing `-` but `:` is more problematic. It has special connotations in image names, for example - allowing it makes it much more ambiguous whether a name refers to an image or container.

@debarshiray The original regex shouldn't have allowed colons, so I would say they are frowned on. Even if we decide to keep support, I would recommend you swap from `:` to a hyphen or underscore, for container/image naming clarity.

@vrothberg You might want to weigh in here as to whether we consider this an API change. IMO this is a bugfix, given that support for non-alphanumeric/underscore characters was never intended and never advertised.

> @vrothberg You might want to weigh in here as to whether we consider this an API change. IMO this is a bugfix, given that support for non-alphanumeric/underscore characters was never intended and never advertised.

Theoretically, it should entail a major version bump as it broke backward compat. The reason for the change should not impact the versioning scheme though.

I am indoctrinated by the kernel's "never break user space" rule. (IMHO) If we allowed `:` before, we should continue to support it. We could discourage users from using a `:` with a big logrus warning (or even error?).

> @debarshiray The original regex shouldn't have allowed colons, so I would say they are frowned on. Even if we decide to keep support, I would recommend you swap from `:` to a hyphen or underscore, for container/image naming clarity.

While I'm not privy on Go's regex quirks, the original regex doesn't check for the beginning and end of the string, so in order to match it would only need to match somewhere in the name, not the name as a whole. The newer regex tests against the whole name.

So with the old regex `fedora-toolbox-me:30` should return two matches `fedora-toolbox-me` and `30`.
With the new regex, the same string will return no matches because of the existence of the colon.
@zerotri Yeah, I think that was an oversight when we wrote the original one... The original intent was to validate the entire name

In the interest of not breaking toolbox, I'm thinking that we'll change the regex to allow `:` characters, with a warning that this is a deprecated feature and may be removed in the future.
@vrothberg @debarshiray Does this sound acceptable?

@mheon That sounds sane to me, but I'd still update toolbox to not use a colon as soon as possible.

I haven't yet been able to fully assess the extent of the breakage here. So far I only know that `podman create` errors out with faulty container names, but I don't know about `podman start` and `podman exec`. If those aren't affected then existing containers would continue to work.

> @vrothberg @debarshiray Does this sound acceptable?

Sounds good to me.

We only validate for creating new containers, so existing ones should be
fine to continue using.

On Thu, Apr 11, 2019, 03:59 Valentin Rothberg <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> @vrothberg <https://github.com/vrothberg> @debarshiray
> <https://github.com/debarshiray> Does this sound acceptable?
>
> Sounds good to me.
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/2876#issuecomment-482010277>,
> or mute the thread
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AHYHCNv9PGS3dJqZ5hbyl55GGQG5qemzks5vfut3gaJpZM4clA2s>
> .
>
I have [updated](https://github.com/debarshiray/toolbox/pull/113) the toolbox to not put colons in the names of containers while creating them.
Updating the regex to allow colons now
I'm going to do this only for containers - pods and volumes will continue to be colon-free
#2914 to re-add `:` as an allowed character with warnings.
